one_line_thesis: Archer is a pre-commercial electric air-taxi developer burning roughly $600 million per year while pursuing FAA type certification for Midnight, scaling manufacturing in California and Georgia, and building defense partnerships.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Archer is a pre-commercial electric air-taxi developer burning roughly $600 million per year while pursuing FAA type certification for Midnight, scaling manufacturing in California and Georgia, and building defense partnerships. FY2025 revenue was $0.3 million; Q1 2026 revenue was $1.6 million, mostly non-recurring. The balance sheet dominates today: about $1.78 billion of liquid assets against debt near $80 million, but diluted share count rose from 624 million to 767 million in one year.

At about $5.31 per share, we expect -3.3% per year over seven years on the scenario base case. Proof-...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Scarce assets, royalties, and optionality
applicability_tests: The security owns a legally enforceable economic claim on scarce assets or royalties.
Material value is omitted or poorly represented by current GAAP earnings.
Asset quantities, ownership, overlap, timing, and remaining capital can be reconciled.
Comparable values can be adjusted for location, quality, control, liquidity, and realization time.
failure_modes: gross asset value mistaken for shareholder value
operating cash flow and NAV counted twice
remote options treated as contracted cash

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

economic_value.accounting_reference: FY2025 10-K and Q1 2026 10-Q; GAAP net loss reflects pre-revenue R&D burn and is not owner cash.

economic_claim.description: One diluted ACHR common share, including net liquid assets, risk-adjusted Midnight certification and partner milestone value, less dilution and burn reserve.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Pre-revenue developer with no operating enterprise cash flow; net liquid assets, certification option, and burn reserve are separate additive components with unique overlap keys.

valuation_proof.evidence: Primary bridge from ACHR/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-Q_20260511_rpt20260331_acc0001824502_26_000038.htm: cash $951.1M, short-term investments $824.8M, debt $80.2M; FY2025 net loss $618.2M per ACHR/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260302_rpt20251231_acc0001824502_26_000019.htm; contract backfill 2026-07-21.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Separate from net liquid claim; does not double-count cash already marked.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

timeline.label: 424B5 equity offering filed; continued use of ATM/S-3 capacity to fund certification and manufacturing scale-up
FY2025 10-K filed: net loss $618.2M on $0.3M revenue; cash $1.02B plus $943M short-term investments at year-end
Hawthorne Airport acquisition closed to support Los Angeles vertiport and test operations
Follow-on 424B5 offering; share count dilution continues ahead of commercial ramp
Proxy and governance cycle; board and compensation framework for pre-commercial phase
Stellantis PIPE financing closed; strategic investor stake and manufacturing partnership
FCA US LLC (Stellantis) initial SC 13D filed; strategic holder disclosed
Founders Adam Goldstein and Brett Adcock file SC 13D; insider alignment at go-public
Archer Aviation closes SPAC merger with Atlas Crest; begins public company reporting as ACHR
Company pivots to Midnight eVTOL air-taxi aircraft; FAA certification path becomes central value driver

industry.structure: Early-stage electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) developers compete for FAA type certification, vertiport infrastructure, and airline or OEM partnerships; no incumbent earns scale commercial air-taxi revenue yet.
industry.share_shift: Archer, Joby, and Beta compete for U.S. Air Force contracts and airline conditional orders; Stellantis-backed manufacturing differentiates Archer versus pure-play peers on production scale.
industry.trend: Regulatory path toward Part 135 commercial operations is the gating item; capital markets fund multi-year certification and factory build-out while revenue remains de minimis.
